calendar.matters:
ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Dec-05-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Sep-19-2025 Continued For Letter To Dec-05-25 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for an Order of Examination as to Judgment Debtor The Burke Group.

Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: D. Carter; Bailiff: Not Reported. (D505)

Judgment Creditor Creditor's Adjustment Bureau, Inc. is present represented by William Abbott.
Judgment Debtor The Burke Group is not present.

The court issues a bench letter and warns if no appearance at the next calling a warrant can be requested by the Judgment Creditor.

This matter is continued to December 5, 2025 at 11:00am in dept. 505.

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Jul-23-2025 Continued To Sep-19-25 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter is on calendar for an Order of Examination filed by the Judgment Creditor Creditor's Adjustment Bureau, Inc.

Judgment Creditor Creditor's Adjustment Bureau is present represented by Theresia Sandhu.
Judgment Debtor The Burke Group is present represented by Luke Stickney.

Order of Examination is conducted.

Parties both stipulate to a continuance of this matter to September 19, 2025 at 9:00am in dept. 505 for further examination.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-09-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-25-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for a request for default judgment against Defendant The Burke Group (A Corporation) DBA Burke Extracts

Dept: 505: Judge: Dennis Hayashi; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" commencing at 9:53:36 a.m. and concluding 10:07:19 a.m. (D505)

Eric Jun, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff Creditor Adjustment Bureau, Inc.
Defendant The Burke Group (A Corporation) DBA Burke Extracts not appearing.
Doug Hewey, sworn to testify for the Plaintiff.

Counsel examines witness. Witness testifies.
Plaintiff submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: 1st contract
Exhibit 2: 2nd contract
Exhibit 3: 3rd contract
Exhibit 4: statement of account

Good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against Defendant as follows:
Damages $46,818.93
Interest at 10% 3,012.70
Costs $585.11
Attorney's Fees $2,825.00
Total Judgment $53,241.74
Proposed judgment signed and executed.
